title: 📈 Macro (Full Story)
content:
📈 Macro (Full Story)
• Rise: Character gets a win.
• Fall: They hit a roadblock.
• Rise: They find hope.
• Fall: It gets worse.
• Climactic rise: They make the final choice.

📉 Micro (One Scene)
• Starts tense.
• Information is revealed.
• Tension gets worse.
• A choice is made.
• Scene ends with a shift.

Activity: Graph Your Story
1. Pick a story idea.
2. Draw a simple wave:
• Mark where things go up and where they go down.
• Look for flat spots—places where the energy doesn’t shift.
• Adjust so that every part of the story moves in some way.

4. Five Essential Story Structures

Different stories fit different structures. Here are five major ones to experiment with:

A. Classic Dramatic Structure (Freytag’s Pyramid)

Best for: Traditional dramas, plays, literary fiction.
